sources.title: NHS App web integration
sources.borrowed: Treat the route as a responsive website surfaced inside the NHS App, with embedded-host chrome suppression but unchanged core semantics.
sources.rejected: Any detached mini-site treatment that would make embedded and browser routes feel like different products.

sources.title: NHS confirmation page pattern
sources.borrowed: Calm transactional hierarchy, next-step clarity, and restrained post-action messaging.
sources.rejected: Large celebratory confirmation styling on a route that is still only in patient-choice posture.

sources.title: NHS question pages
sources.borrowed: One clear decision surface, explicit back/return continuity, and preserving browser history instead of breaking it.
sources.rejected: Form-heavy chrome or multi-question density that would bury the ranked choice stack.

sources.title: WAI-ARIA Radio Group Pattern
sources.borrowed: Radiogroup semantics with checked state and arrow-key movement across options.
sources.rejected: Consumer-card selection without formal keyboard behavior.

sources.title: WAI-ARIA Dialog (Modal) Pattern
sources.borrowed: Focus placement and return expectations for bounded recovery or future modal follow-ons.
sources.rejected: Turning provenance or repair surfaces into blocking dialogs when same-shell panels are sufficient.

sources.title: WCAG 2.2 Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
sources.borrowed: Sticky tray and embedded ribbon reserve enough space for focused controls to remain visible.
sources.rejected: Sticky chrome that clips option cards or action buttons on mobile.

sources.title: WCAG 2.2 Reflow
sources.borrowed: Single-column collapse and no two-dimensional scrolling at 320 CSS pixels.
sources.rejected: Wide card fact layouts that force horizontal scroll.

sources.title: WCAG Status Messages
sources.borrowed: Selection, decline-all, callback, and recovery transitions announce through a polite status region.
sources.rejected: Visual-only status changes that are invisible to assistive technology.

sources.title: Playwright best practices
sources.borrowed: User-visible assertions, stable route markers, and isolated contexts.
sources.rejected: Classname-driven selectors and state leakage between scenarios.

sources.title: Playwright ARIA snapshots
sources.borrowed: Structured accessibility proof for live, blocked, and recovery states.
sources.rejected: Relying on screenshots alone for semantics.

sources.title: Playwright emulation
sources.borrowed: Phone-sized and embedded-host responsive proof using realistic viewport/device settings.
sources.rejected: Desktop-only proof for a route that must hold up inside the NHS App.

sources.title: Playwright trace viewer
sources.borrowed: Trace-first debugging for selection, callback, and recovery navigation.
sources.rejected: History-sensitive test failures without recorded interaction evidence.

sources.title: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
sources.borrowed: Low-noise hierarchy, quiet density, and alignment discipline.
sources.rejected: Desktop productivity chrome that would make the patient route feel like a workspace.

sources.title: Linear concepts
sources.borrowed: Clear saved-context framing and predictable structural grouping.
sources.rejected: Navigation density or view-local complexity beyond what a patient transactional route needs.
